clinical_coherence.notes: 81M East Asian with DM2, HTN, hyperlipidemia, BPH, bilateral cataract surgery. Progressive memory decline over 2 years. MMSE 20/30. Coherent clinical picture for early AD in an elderly male. Age 81 is at the upper range for 'early' AD presentation but still plausible.

tool_output_consistency.notes: All modalities concordant. MRI shows bilateral hippocampal atrophy (Scheltens 3) with moderate temporoparietal cortical volume loss. Neuropsych confirms amnestic profile. Amyloid PET positive. CSF A+T+N+. EEG shows slowed PDR with bitemporal theta. All consistent with mild AD.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD G30.1 correct for late-onset AD. Critical actions comprehensive. Differential appropriately considers age-related cognitive decline and vascular dementia given advanced age and comorbidities.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Appropriately straightforward. Classic amnestic presentation in elderly male. No significant diagnostic ambiguity.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 81M East Asian with the standard PMH template (DM2, HTN, hyperlipidemia, BPH, same medication pattern). The patient profile is plausible but templated. Cataract surgery adds some uniqueness. The age is somewhat advanced for 'early-stage' AD designation but clinically possible if onset was relatively recent.

educational_value.notes: Standard AD case. Limited novelty beyond S01-S03. Age-related considerations (advanced age increasing likelihood of mixed pathology, medication burden) add some value but are not prominently featured.

issues_found: Templated PMH shared with S02 and S03
Limited novelty in the straightforward case set
strengths: Appropriate for advanced age AD presentation
